title: Read more …
content:
Media Releases
Police investigate stabbing
April 3, 2025
Charges are pending against a man from Sydney Mines in connection to a stabbing April 2
nd
.
Cape Breton Regional Police responded to reports of an altercation between two individuals in an apartment complex on Burchell Street Wednesday afternoon; one man was transported to Cape Breton Regional Hospital by EHS for treatment of non-life-threatening injuries, and the second man had already left the scene. Members of the K-9 Team attended the scene to assist, but officers were unable to locate him.  Police continue attempts to locate the second man today.
Both individuals are known to each other, and police are treating this as an isolated incident.

title: Read more …
content:
Media Releases
Police arrest barricaded man for threats
March 24, 2025
A 57-year-old man faces charges after Cape Breton Regional Police safely arrested him from barricading inside a property on Jessie Hardy Lane in Gabarus.
Police responded to a reported altercation between two people on Forchu Road at approximately 7:00 PM March 23
rd
, where one person had assaulted and threatened the other before returning to his neighboring home, where he was believed to have a firearm. Officers located him inside a building on his property, where he refused to exit; Crisis Negotiators were called to assist, and members of the Emergency Response Team attended the property due the potential that the man had a firearm. At approximately 1:00 AM, the man exited his building, was found not to be in possession of a firearm and was arrested.
He was later released on a Promise to Appear in Sydney Provincial Court, charged with Assault and Uttering Threats to Damage Property and Cause Bodily Harm or Death.

title: Read more …
content:
Media Releases
Police investigating fatal vehicle collision
March 24, 2025
Cape Breton Regional Police are investing a single-vehicle collision on the Eskasoni Highway near Islandview March 22
nd
.
Officers were called to respond at approximately 7:30 PM,  where Fire Services and EHS were working at a vehicle in the trees several hundred feet from the roadway that witnesses had helped to locate. EHS pronounced the driver and sole occupant of the vehicle, a 20-year-old male, deceased at the scene.
Traffic Safety investigators attended to process the scene, had the vehicle towed for mechanical inspection and continue to investigate. Our thoughts are with the victim’s family during this difficult time.

title: Read more …
content:
Media Releases
Police seize illegal drugs, lay charges for trafficking
March 18, 2025
Seven people face drugs and weapons charges as part of ongoing Cape Breton Regional Police investigation into illegal drug activity in the CBRM.
Over the last 2 weeks, Street Crime Officers seized Cocaine, prescription pills, a firearm and ammunition from three separate matters: a vehicle stop on Highway 28 in South Bar on March 3
rd
and search warrants under the
Controlled Drugs and Substances Act
at a residence on Water Street in Glace Bay on March 12
th
and Emerald Street in New Waterford on March 17
th
.
Brianna Lynn Carrol (37) and Natasha Marie O’Neil (39) are charged with: Possession for the Purpose of Trafficking in Cocaine and Possession for the Purpose of Trafficking in Methylphenidate as well as several Breaches of previous Court-ordered conditions.
Ryan Joseph Munden (44) and Sheldon Christopher MacLean (39) are charged with: Possession for the Purpose of Trafficking in Cocaine, Hydromorphone, Buprenorphine, Methylphenidate and Possession of Property Obtained by Crime; Munden is also charged with Failure to Attend Court and MacLean with two Breaches of Probation.
John Blair Tighe (39), Kristen Nicole Reid (34) and Matthew Lovell (30) are charged with Possession for the Purpose of Trafficking in Cocaine, Possession of Property Obtained by Crime, Unauthorized Possession of a Firearm and with Knowledge, and Careless Use of a Firearm and Ammunition. Tighe is also charged with several Breaches of previous Court-ordered conditions.

title: Read more …
content:
Media Releases
Police catch, charge driver who ran from collision
March 18, 2025
A 36-year-old man man faces charges after Cape Breton Regional Police caught him on the run from a collision in Sydney on March 16th.
Police responded to a two-vehicle collision on Cottage Road at approximately 6:30 PM, where witnesses reported seeing a man and woman run from one of the vehicles. Officers patrolled the nearby area and spotted people matching the description of the vehicle occupants on Rockdale Ave; when they saw police, the man tried to run away, but officers caught him and arrested him. Noting signs of impairment, officers also requested a breath sample which the man refused. The occupant of the second vehicle was treated for non-life-threatening injuries.
After further investigation, the female was released without charges. John William Aaron Hickey is charged with Failure to Stop after an Accident, Failure to Comply with a Demand, Resisting a Peace Officer, and Failure to Attend Court on previous charges.

title: Read more …
content:
Media Releases
Police investigating possible shots fired in Sydney Mines
March 10, 2025
Cape Breton Regional Police are looking for people who may have information about shots fired on Cottage Street in Sydney Mines Monday morning.
Officers responded to a call from residents at approximately 7:30 AM, after they discovered damage to their house that looked like bullet holes. Forensic Identification officers assisted with collecting evidence from the residence, and with information from the homeowners, police were able to confirm that shots were fired from a vehicle driving on Cottage Road, between 4:30 and 5:00 AM. It is described as a dark-coloured hatchback (photo attached) with a loud muffler, that was also seen traveling on Pitt towards Legatto Street.
Police would like to speak to anyone who has video – in addition to what has already been reviewed – from the neighbourhood around Cottage Street, between the hours of 3:30 and 6:00 AM this morning, March 10
th
, as well as anyone who may have seen or heard anything during this time period or who has information about this incident. Please call police at 902-563-5151 or anonymously through Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-8477 or
https://crimestoppers.ns.ca
.

title: Read more …
content:
Media Releases
Man faces weapons charges after caught hunting at night
March 7, 2025
Cape Breton Regional Police have charged a man with weapons offences, after they caught him hunting unlawfully in the early morning hours of February 27
th
.
Officers responded to reports of suspicious activity in Grand Narrows, where a resident had seen a person with a flashlight and heard a gunshot, suspecting that someone was hunting. The resident was able to provide police with a vehicle description, and officers patrolled the area locating the vehicle on Grand Narrows Highway, where they stopped the driver and found a firearm and a deer inside the vehicle.
Police seized the weapon and arrested the man; through further investigation in conjunction with Natural Resources Conservation Officer Service, officers seized additional firearms, ammunition and hunted animals from a residence.
The 52-year-old man was released on a Promise to Appear in Sydney Provincial Court on April 1
st
, charged with Possession of a Weapon for a Dangerous Purpose and Careless Use of a Firearm. Natural Resources Conservation Officer Service continues to investigate potential violations under the
Wildlife Act
.
Police and Natural Resources encourage the public to continue to report any suspicious hunting activity and suspected wildlife violations to 1-800-565-2224.

title: Media Releases
content:

April 10, 2025

Staff evacuated the mall while police searched the building and later cleared it with no risk to public safety.

Police investigating assault
March 13, 2025
Cape Breton Regional Police are looking for help to identify a person involved in a March 3
rd
assault at the intersection of George and Prince Streets in Sydney.
Police were called to the area at approximately 1:20 PM, where witnesses indicated a man had been punched and kicked by another man who then walked away up Prince Street; officers patrolled the area and were unable to locate anyone matching his description. The male victim was transported by EHS to Cape Breton Regional Hospital for treatment of serious injuries, where he remains now in stable condition.
Through further investigation, police have obtained video surveillance of the assault and are looking for the second person involved, who is described as a man in his 30s who was wearing a grey jacket and blue camouflage pants. If you can help identify this person, or have any additional information about the incident, please contact police at 902-563-5151 or anonymously through Crime Stoppers.
Deceased person located in Sydney
March 12, 2025
Cape Breton Regional Police investigators located a deceased female in the area of Glenwood Street in Sydney Tuesday afternoon, March 11
th
, as part of ongoing investigation into a reported missing person.
Members of the Forensic Identification Unit examined the scene; there is nothing to indicate foul play is a factor in this death, and the deceased has been referred to the Medical Examiner’s Office for autopsy to confirm the cause of death. The person’s identity will not be released at this time, pending completion of the Medical Examiner’s work and notification of next of kin.

title: Missing Persons
content:
Missing Persons
Cape Breton Regional Police continue to investigate open cases of missing persons in our area.
If you have any information about any of these cases, that could help lead police to the whereabouts of these individuals, please contact police at 902-563-5151 or report tips anonymously through CrimeStoppers at 902-562-8477 or online at
Cape Breton Crime Stoppers
.
Kenneth Justin MacDonald
Date of Birth
: May 31, 1990
Location
: North Sydney
Justin MacDonald was last seen on July 7th, 2024 at approximately 11 PM at the Irving gas station located at 183 Commercial Street, North Sydney, where he was captured on store video. Police later located Justin’s vehicle, a blue Kia Forte, NS License Plate #FYZ-715 approximately 25 km away in the Ormond Crescent area of Mira Road, in CBRM.  Justin is described as 5’9” tall and 240 pounds with sandy blond hair and blue eyes.
Mark Joseph MacIsaac
Date of Birth
: July 15, 1981
Location
: Sydney
Mark MacIsaac previously resided in Scotchtown and was most recently believed to be residing in the Sydney area, but there is no information his confirmed whereabouts. Mark also uses the nickname "Pony" and is described as 5’ 8” tall and 165 pounds with brown hair, brown eyes and a “NWB” tattoo on his left bicep.
Alphonsus O'Brien
Date of Birth
: August 29, 1926
Location
: Glace Bay
Alphonse O'Brien did not return from a walk outside his house on April 22nd, 2020, which is near the end of 8th Street in Glace Bay and near cliffs and the shoreline. He was wearing a black jacket with no shirt underneath, brown pants and boots. With assistance from Ground Search and Rescue, Marine and Fire Rescue, and Military and DNR helicopters, all ground and water search efforts were exhausted.
Micheal Gerald Steele
Date of Birth
: March 14, 1980
Location
: Sydney
Micheal Gerald Steele has not been seen or heard from since family last had contact with him on March 14th, 2019. He is described as 5’ 10” and 155 pounds with brown hair and blue eyes. Police and family have exhausted all available information in attempting to determine his whereabouts and hope that someone can provide new information to follow up on.
Kenneth Douglas MacNeil
Date of Birth
: November 24, 1963
Location
: Leitches Creek
Kenneth Douglas MacNeil was last seen at his Leitches Creek Road residence on August 27, 2014, at approximately 5:00 PM. He is described as approximately 5’5” tall and 120 pounds, with a slim build and grey/black hair which is below his ears and above his shoulders. Kenneth was last seen wearing green flower shorts and a grey t-shirt.
Steven MacNeil
Date of Birth
: December 29, 1960
Location
: Sydney
Steven MacNeil was last seen on the afternoon of November 28, 2010, leaving his Charlotte Street residence on his bike, and he was reported missing on November 30th, after he had not yet returned. He's described as approximately 5' 9" tall with a stocky build and curly black hair. At the time he left his residence, he was we4aring blue jeans and a blue and white jacket; his bike was red and white.
Ian Douglas Murray
Date of Birth
: August 4, 1977
Location
: Sydney
Ian Douglass Murray has been missing since March 2, 2010. Mr. Murray was last seen wearing dark blue jeans, rolled up, and a brown checker sip-up hoody. He is described as 5' 3" tall, weighing 145 pounds with dark brown hair, and he sometimes wears a goatee. Ian may also be wearing a hat down low on his face.
David John Hinchey
Date of Birth
: August 15, 1943
Location
: New Waterford
